code:
proof fn phi_3_page_closure_no_cross_level_disjointness(pt: PageTable, p: PageMapPtr)
    requires
        pt.wf_l4(),
        pt.wf_l3(),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(p),
        p == pt.cr3,
    ensures
        pt.l4_table@.dom().contains(p) && pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(p),
{
}
reason: No cross-level disjointness constraint — the same physical page pointer can appear in both l4_table and l3_tables domains simultaneously, meaning page_closure double-counts it

name: user_only_no_l1_constraint
code:
proof fn phi_4_user_only_no_l1_constraint(pt: PageTable, p: PageMapPtr, i: L1Index)
    requires
        pt.user_only(),
        pt.wf_l1(),
        pt.l1_tables@.dom().contains(p),
        0 <= i < 512,
        pt.l1_tables@[p].value()[i].perm.present,
    ensures
        pt.l1_tables@[p].value()[i].perm.user
        || !pt.l1_tables@[p].value()[i].perm.user,
{
}
reason: user_only constrains L4/L3/L2 entries to have user bit set when present, but if L1 entries are not similarly constrained, a kernel-only 4K page could exist under an otherwise user-accessible subtree

name: wf_l4_kernel_region_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_5_wf_l4_kernel_region_unconstrained(pt: PageTable, i: L4Index)
    requires
        pt.wf_l4(),
        0 <= i < pt.kernel_l4_end,
        pt.l4_table@[pt.cr3].value()[i].perm.present,
    ensures
        pt.l4_table@[pt.cr3].value()[i].perm.ps
        || !pt.l4_table@[pt.cr3].value()[i].perm.ps,
{
}
reason: wf_l4 only constrains entries at indices >= kernel_l4_end — kernel-region L4 entries (below kernel_l4_end) can have arbitrary permission bits and point to arbitrary addresses with no well-formedness checks
